channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: NEET UG 2026 Results: Over 11.21 Lakh Candidates Qualify as Women Record Higher Success Rate Than Men
item.description: The National Testing Agency (NTA) declared the results of the NEET UG 2026 examination on July 16 following the re-examination conducted on June 21. The retest was organised after the original examina...

item.title: Bow Glacier Falls Trail Reopens with Safety Precautions After 2025 Rock Slide
item.description: The Bow Glacier Falls Trail in Banff National Park has reopened to hikers following a 2025 rockslide that killed two individuals, Jutta Hinrichs and Hamza Benhilal. Parks Canada implemented temporary ...

item.title: Mortgage Rates Reach Multi-Month High Amid Economic Uncertainty
item.description: Mortgage rates in the United States have surged to their highest level in nearly 12 months, reflecting growing concerns about inflation and economic stability. According to data from the Mortgage Bank...

item.title: Kenyan Artist Frustrated After Girlfriend Floods Home and Damages Artwork
item.description: A Kenyan university student and artist shared a distressing video showing his home flooded after a domestic argument with his girlfriend. The man walked through his damaged house, highlighting canvase...

item.title: Chanda's Acting Skills Shine as She Accepts Melai's Challenge in "Kuan on One"
item.description: The article highlights Chanda Romero's versatility as an actress, showcasing her ability to portray both playful and serious roles in the TV series "Kuan on One." Melai Cantiveros, her co-star, challe...

item.title: Report Details Allegations About Sen. Ruben Gallego's Past Consensual Relationships With House Staffers
item.description: A New York Post report alleges that Arizona Democratic Sen. Ruben Gallego, who is widely viewed as a possible 2028 presidential candidate, had consensual romantic relationships with at least two congr...

item.title: Pakistan joins global AI cooperation initiative to shape future tech policies
item.description: Pakistan has officially become a founding member of the World Artificial Intelligence Cooperation Organisation (WAICO), marking a significant step in its efforts to influence global AI governance. The...

item.title: Andhra Pradesh Govt. to Boost Handloom and Handicraft Sectors with Market Expansion and Innovation Support
item.description: Minister S. Savitha, heading the Department of Handlooms and Textiles, reiterated the Andhra Pradesh government's commitment to bolstering the state's handloom and handicraft sectors. During her visit...

item.title: Tanzania Aims to Ensure Clean Water Access for All Villages by 2031
item.description: Tanzania has announced an ambitious plan to achieve universal access to clean and safe water across all villages by 2031. This initiative, unveiled at the African Water Forum in N’Djamena, Chad, align...

item.title: Kai Trump Updates on Mother's Breast Cancer Battle
item.description: Kai Trump, 18, the eldest grandchild of US President Donald Trump, shared an update on her mother Vanessa Trump's health as she continues fighting breast cancer. The young Trump spoke openly about her...

item.title: Stock Market Rises Amid Inflation Data and Tech Sector Volatility
item.description: The stock market experienced mixed performance on Wednesday as the Dow Jones Industrial Average and other major indexes closed higher, driven by an unexpected decline in wholesale inflation. Gasoline ...

item.title: South Africa's Presidency Denies Isolation Claims Amid Anti-Migration Protests
item.description: The South African Presidency has firmly rejected allegations that the country is isolated from the African continent, dismissing them as part of a sustained misinformation campaign. Presidential Spoke...

item.title: New laws mandate Australian companies to prevent modern slavery with penalties
item.description: The Australian government is introducing stricter measures to combat modern slavery, requiring large companies with annual revenue over A$100 million to prevent exploitative practices. These reforms f...

item.title: Residents Evacuated as Wildfire Threatens Boston Bar, B.C.
item.description: Residents of Boston Bar, B.C., have been ordered to evacuate due to a wildfire that flared overnight, according to the Fraser Valley Regional District. Evacuees are advised to head south via Highway 1...

item.title: Managing Climate Anxiety: Effective Strategies for Coping
item.description: The article explores climate anxiety, a growing concern among Canadians experiencing distress related to environmental threats. Dr. Jennifer Caspari outlines practical strategies to manage this condit...

item.title: Rising Fresh Produce Prices and Consumer Strategies
item.description: The article examines the factors driving up the cost of fresh fruits and vegetables in the U.S., including extreme weather events, labor shortages, rising energy and shipping costs, and trade policy c...

item.title: Nurse Mary Died While Providing Health Care to Works Minister
item.description: Nurse Mary Habila, a dedicated healthcare professional, passed away while serving as part of the medical team supporting Minister David Umahi. The Executive Chairman of Kaduna State's Jaba Local Gover...

item.title: Phoebe Cates' Turbulent Experience on 'Paradise' and Her Rise to Stardom
item.description: Phoebe Cates' experience on the set of her debut film 'Paradise' (1982) was marked by significant stress and regret. At 17, she transitioned from modeling to acting, accepting a lead role after her fa...

item.title: Stanbic Bank Kenya Appoints Michael Mutiga as New CEO with 20+ Years of Experience
item.description: Stanbic Bank Kenya has appointed Michael Mutiga as its new Chief Executive Officer, effective August 1, 2026. The Board of Directors highlighted his extensive experience spanning over two decades in b...

item.title: Bandits attack Ankpechi community in Benue State, residents flee
item.description: Residents of Ankpechi community in Benue State fled their homes after armed bandits launched a sudden attack on Thursday morning. The attackers, identified as armed herdsmen, stormed the quiet communi...
